paragraphs.zh: 我们像多米诺骨牌一样倒下。
我遇到了维罗妮卡修女。

paragraphs.en: I’m afraid she has a bit of a bloody nose.
paragraphs.zh: 我担心她的鼻子有点流血。

paragraphs.en: I’m beginning to think you’re punching people.
Never mind. Well, come in, please.
I actually have a hot pot of tea.
And close this, but not quite for form’s sake.
paragraphs.zh: 关闭它，但不完全是为了形式。

paragraphs.en: Father, would you care for a…
Would you have a cup of tea, Father?
I’d love a cup of tea.
Sister, perhaps you could serve him.
paragraphs.zh: 姐姐，也许你可以为他服务。

paragraphs.en: Would you like a cup of tea,
No, I’ve already had my cup.
paragraphs.zh: 不，我已经喝完了我的杯子。
是的，是的。这里的某个地方。

paragraphs.en: I put it in the drawer for Lent last year.
paragraphs.zh: 去年四旬斋时我把它放在抽屉里。

paragraphs.en: Never remembered to take it out.
It mustn’t have been much to give up, then.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，应该没有什么值得放弃的。

paragraphs.en: I’ll serve you, although for want of practice I’m a little clumsy.
paragraphs.zh: 我会为你服务，尽管由于缺乏练习，我有点笨拙。

paragraphs.en: Not that there’s anything wrong with sugar.
paragraphs.zh: 并不是说糖有什么问题。

paragraphs.en: Well, thank you, Father, for making time for us.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，父亲，谢谢您为我们腾出时间。

paragraphs.en: We are at our wit’s end.
I think it’s an excellent idea to rethink the Christmas pageant,
paragraphs.zh: 我认为重新考虑圣诞节选美是个好主意，

paragraphs.en: ’cause last year’s effort was a little woebegone.
paragraphs.zh: 因为去年的努力有点令人沮丧。

paragraphs.en: Oh, I loved it, but I love all pageants.
paragraphs.zh: 哦，我喜欢它，但我喜欢所有的选美比赛。

paragraphs.en: O Little Town of Bethlehem, O Come,

paragraphs.zh: 哦，来吧，以马内利，哦，来吧……

paragraphs.en: So what do you think, Father?
Is there something new we could do?
paragraphs.zh: 我们可以做一些新的事情吗？

paragraphs.en: Well, we all love the Christmas hymns, but it might be jolly to include a secular song.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，我们都喜欢圣诞赞美诗，但加入一首世俗歌曲可能会很高兴。

paragraphs.en: It’s Beginning To Look A Lot

paragraphs.zh: 就像圣诞节一样。类似的事情。

paragraphs.en: What would be the point of a secular song?
paragraphs.zh: 一首世俗歌曲的意义何在？

paragraphs.en: We could have one of the boys dress up as a snowman, dance around.
paragraphs.zh: 我们可以让其中一个男孩装扮成雪人，跳舞。

paragraphs.en: Frosty the Snowman espouses a pagan belief in magic.
paragraphs.zh: 雪人霜拥护异教对魔法的信仰。

paragraphs.en: The snowman comes to life when an enchanted hat is put on his head.
paragraphs.zh: 当一顶魔法帽子戴在雪人头上时，雪人就活了。

paragraphs.en: If the music were more somber, people would realize the images are disturbing and the song heretical.
paragraphs.zh: 如果音乐更加阴郁，人们就会意识到图像令人不安，歌曲是异端。

paragraphs.en: It should be banned from the airwaves.
paragraphs.zh: 应该禁止它在广播中传播。

paragraphs.en: Oh, It’s Beginning To Look A Lot Like
Christmas would be fine, I suppose.
May I ask what you’re writing down with that ballpoint pen?
paragraphs.zh: 请问你用那支圆珠笔写了什么？

paragraphs.en: Oh, nothing. It’s an idea for a sermon.
paragraphs.zh: 哦，没什么。这是一个布道的想法。

paragraphs.en: Oh. You had one right now?
I get them all the time.
I forget them, so I have to write them down.
paragraphs.zh: 我忘记了它们，所以我必须把它们写下来。

paragraphs.en: Would you like a little more tea, Father?
paragraphs.zh: 爸爸，您想再喝一点茶吗？
这是一个新的时代，姐妹。

paragraphs.en: Something inside of people is new.
paragraphs.zh: 人们内心的某些东西是新的。

paragraphs.en: There is nothing new under the sun.
We should sing a song from the radio now and then, take the kids out for ice cream.
paragraphs.zh: 我们应该时不时地唱一首收音机里的歌，带孩子们出去吃冰淇淋。

paragraphs.en: And maybe take the boys on a camping trip.
paragraphs.zh: 也许还可以带孩子们去露营。

paragraphs.en: You know, the children and the parents should see us as members of their family.
paragraphs.zh: 您知道，孩子和父母应该将我们视为家庭成员。

paragraphs.en: But we are not members of their family.
paragraphs.zh: 但我们不是他们的家庭成员。
为什么？因为我们的誓言？

paragraphs.en: I don’t think we’re so different.
paragraphs.zh: 我不认为我们有什么不同。

paragraphs.en: I would take some more tea.
And they think we’re different, yeah?
paragraphs.zh: 他们认为我们与众不同，是吗？

paragraphs.en: The working class people of this parish trust us to be different.
paragraphs.zh: 这个教区的工人阶级相信我们与众不同。

paragraphs.en: I think we’re getting off the subject.
paragraphs.zh: 我想我们已经偏离主题了。
是的，你说得对。你说得对。

paragraphs.en: Well, you tell her to wash her face and her neck and report to me at 3:00.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，你让她洗把脸和脖子，3:00 来给我报到。
打扰一下。圣诞节选美比赛。

paragraphs.en: We must be careful how Donald Miller is used in the pageant.
paragraphs.zh: 我们必须小心唐纳德·米勒在选美比赛中的使用方式。

paragraphs.en: All right, what about Donald Miller?
We must be careful, in the pageant, that we neither hide Donald Miller nor put him forward.
paragraphs.zh: 在选美比赛中，我们必须小心，既不要隐藏唐纳德·米勒，也不要把他推到前面。

paragraphs.en: Because of the color of his skin?
I think he should be treated like every other boy.
paragraphs.zh: 我认为他应该像其他男孩一样受到对待。

paragraphs.en: Well, you yourself singled the boy out for special attention.
paragraphs.zh: 嗯，你自己挑出了这个男孩来特别关注。

paragraphs.en: You held a private meeting with him at the rectory. A week ago?
paragraphs.zh: 你在教区长官邸与他举行了一次私人会面。一周前？

paragraphs.en: The boy acted strangely when he returned to class.
paragraphs.zh: 这个男孩回到教室后表现得很奇怪。

paragraphs.en: When he returned from the rectory, a little odd, yes.
paragraphs.zh: 当他从教区长官邸回来时，是的，有点奇怪。
你能告诉我们为什么吗？
他怎么表现得很奇怪呢？

paragraphs.en: I’m not sure how to explain it.
He laid his head on the desk and…
paragraphs.zh: 他把头靠在桌子上，然后……

paragraphs.en: Do you mean you had some impression?
And he’d come from the rectory, so you’re asking me?
paragraphs.zh: 他来自教区长官邸，所以你问我？

paragraphs.en: Did you want to discuss the pageant?

paragraphs.zh: 这就是我来这里的原因吗？

paragraphs.en: Or is this what you wanted to discuss?
paragraphs.zh: 或者这就是你想讨论的？

paragraphs.en: The boy’s well-being is my responsibility.
paragraphs.zh: 孩子的健康是我的责任。

paragraphs.en: His well-being is not at issue.
I’m not satisfied that that is true.
He was upset when he returned to class.
paragraphs.zh: 当他回到课堂时，他很沮丧。
教区长府里发生了什么？

paragraphs.en: I had a talk with the boy.
This is not about my tone or your tone, Father Flynn.
paragraphs.zh: 这与我的语气或你的语气无关，弗林神父。

paragraphs.en: It’s about arriving at the truth.
You know what I’m talking about, don’t you? Hmm?
paragraphs.zh: 你知道我在说什么，不是吗？唔？

paragraphs.en: And you’re controlling the expression on your face right now, aren’t you?
paragraphs.zh: 你现在正在控制自己脸上的表情，不是吗？

paragraphs.en: What exactly are you accusing me of?
I’m not accusing you of anything,
I am asking you to tell me what happened in the rectory.
paragraphs.zh: 我请你告诉我教区长住宅里发生了什么事。

paragraphs.en: I don’t wish to continue this.
And if you’re dissatisfied with that,
I suggest you speak to Monsignor Benedict.
paragraphs.zh: 我建议你和本尼迪克特主教谈谈。

paragraphs.en: I can only imagine this unfortunate behavior is the result of overwork.
paragraphs.zh: 我只能想象这种不幸的行为是过度劳累的结果。

paragraphs.en: Have a good morning, Sister. Sister.
There was alcohol on his breath when he returned from his meeting with you.
paragraphs.zh: 当他和你见面回来时，他的呼吸里充满了酒精。

paragraphs.en: I did smell it on his breath.
paragraphs.zh: 我确实从他的呼吸中闻到了这种味道。

paragraphs.en: Would you like some more tea?
Mr. McGuinn caught Donald drinking altar wine.
paragraphs.zh: 麦吉恩先生发现唐纳德正在喝祭坛酒。

paragraphs.en: When I found out, I sent for him.
paragraphs.zh: 当我知道后，我派人去找他。

paragraphs.en: There were tears, and he begged not to be removed from the altar boys. I took pity on him.
paragraphs.zh: 他流下了眼泪，恳求不要将他从祭坛男孩中除名。我可怜他。

paragraphs.en: I told him if no one else found out,
paragraphs.zh: 我告诉他如果没有人发现的话

paragraphs.en: What a relief. That explains everything.
paragraphs.zh: 真是一种解脱。这就解释了一切。

paragraphs.en: Look, Sister, it was all a mistake.
paragraphs.zh: 姐姐，你看，这一切都是一个错误。

paragraphs.en: And if I talk to Mr. McGuinn?
paragraphs.zh: 如果我和麦吉恩先生谈谈呢？

paragraphs.en: Talk to him, by all means.
But now that the boy’s secret’s out,
paragraphs.zh: 但现在男孩的秘密已经泄露了

paragraphs.en: I’m gonna have to remove him from the altar boys.
paragraphs.zh: 我得把他从祭坛男孩中除掉。

paragraphs.en: That’s what I was trying to avoid.
You were trying to protect the boy.
I might have done the same thing.
paragraphs.zh: 我可能也做了同样的事情。

paragraphs.en: Is there a way Donald could stay on the altar boys?
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德有办法继续留在祭坛男孩吗？

paragraphs.en: If the boy drank altar wine, he can’t continue as an altar boy.
paragraphs.zh: 如果男孩喝了坛酒，他就不能继续当坛童。

paragraphs.en: I have some writing to do.
I’m not pleased with how you handled this.
paragraphs.zh: 我对你处理这件事的方式不满意。

paragraphs.en: What a relief. He cleared it all up.
paragraphs.zh: 真是一种解脱。他把一切都清理干净了。

paragraphs.en: Isn’t it that it’s easier to believe him?
But we can corroborate his story with Mr. McGuinn.
paragraphs.zh: 但我们可以与麦吉恩先生证实他的故事。

paragraphs.en: These types of people are clever.
You just want things to be resolved so you can have simplicity back.
paragraphs.zh: 你只是希望事情得到解决，这样你就能恢复简单。

paragraphs.en: I want no further part of this.
How can you be so sure that he is lying?
paragraphs.zh: 你怎么能这么肯定他在撒谎？

paragraphs.en: You don’t like it that he uses a ballpoint pen.
You don’t like it that he takes three lumps of sugar in his tea.
paragraphs.zh: 你不喜欢他在茶里放三块糖。

paragraphs.en: You don’t like it that he likes Frosty the Snowman, and you are letting that convince you of something terrible, just terrible.
paragraphs.zh: 你不喜欢他喜欢雪人霜，你让这让你相信一些可怕的事情，非常可怕。

paragraphs.en: Well, I like Frosty the Snowman!
And I think it would be nice if this school weren’t run like a jail, and I think it’s a good thing that I love to teach history and that I might inspire my students to love it, too.
paragraphs.zh: 我认为如果这所学校不像监狱一样运作就好了，我认为我喜欢教历史是一件好事，而且我可能会激励我的学生也喜欢它。

paragraphs.en: And if you judge that to mean that I am not fit to be a teacher, then so be it!
paragraphs.zh: 如果你认为我不适合当老师，那就这样吧！

paragraphs.en: Look at that. You blew out my light.
paragraphs.zh: 看看那个。你吹灭了我的灯。

paragraphs.en: In ancient Sparta, important matters were decided by who shouted loudest.
paragraphs.zh: 在古代斯巴达，重要的事情是由谁喊得最大声来决定的。

paragraphs.en: Fortunately, we are not in ancient Sparta.
paragraphs.zh: 幸运的是，我们不是在古代斯巴达。

paragraphs.en: You honestly find the students in this school to be treated like inmates in a prison?
paragraphs.zh: 你真的觉得这所学校的学生就像监狱里的囚犯一样吗？

paragraphs.en: Actually, they all seem fairly happy, but they’re all uniformly terrified of you.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，他们看起来都很高兴，但他们都一致害怕你。
是的。这就是它的工作原理。

paragraphs.en: Hello, this is Sister Aloysius Beauvier, principal at St. Nicholas School.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，我是阿洛伊修斯·博维尔修女，圣尼古拉斯学校的校长。

paragraphs.en: I’m calling about your son, Donald.
paragraphs.zh: 我打电话来是为了你儿子唐纳德。

paragraphs.en: A woman was gossiping with a friend about a man she hardly knew.
paragraphs.zh: 一个女人正在和一个朋友谈论一个她几乎不认识的男人。

paragraphs.en: I know none of you have ever done this.
paragraphs.zh: 我知道你们都没有这样做过。

paragraphs.en: That night she had a dream.
paragraphs.zh: 那天晚上，她做了一个梦。

paragraphs.en: A great hand appeared over her and pointed down at her.
paragraphs.zh: 一只大手出现在她上方，向下指着她。

paragraphs.en: She was immediately seized with an overwhelming sense of guilt.
paragraphs.zh: 她顿时涌上一股强烈的愧疚感。

paragraphs.en: The next day she went to confession.
She got the old parish priest,
She told him the whole thing.
paragraphs.zh: 她把事情的全部经过都告诉了他。

paragraphs.en: “Is gossiping a sin?” she asked the old man.
“Was that the hand of God Almighty pointing a finger at me?
paragraphs.zh: “那是全能神的手在指着我吗？

paragraphs.en: “Should I be asking your absolution, Father?
paragraphs.zh: “我应该请求您的宽恕吗，神父？

paragraphs.en: “Tell me, have I done something wrong?”
paragraphs.zh: “告诉我，我是不是做错了什么？”

paragraphs.en: “Yes, you ignorant, badly brought up female.
paragraphs.zh: “是啊，你这个无知、没教养的女人。

paragraphs.en: “You have borne false witness against your neighbor.
“You have played fast and loose with his reputation,
So the woman said she was sorry and asked for forgiveness.
paragraphs.zh: 于是，女子表示歉意并请求原谅。

paragraphs.en: Take a pillow up on your roof,
“cut it open with a knife, and return here to me.”
paragraphs.zh: “用刀把它切开，然后回到我这里来。”

paragraphs.en: So the woman went home, took a pillow off her bed, a knife from the drawer, went up the fire escape to her roof and stabbed the pillow.
paragraphs.zh: 于是，这名女子回到家，从床上取下一个枕头，从抽屉里拿出一把刀，从防火梯爬到屋顶，刺伤了枕头。

paragraphs.en: Then she went back to the old parish priest as instructed.
paragraphs.zh: 然后她按照指示回到了老教区牧师那里。

paragraphs.en: “Did you gut the pillow with a knife?” he says.
paragraphs.zh: “你用刀把枕头掏空了吗？”他说。

paragraphs.en: “Now, I want you to go back
“and gather up every last feather that flew out on the wind.”
paragraphs.zh: “把随风飞出的每一根羽毛都收集起来。”

paragraphs.en: The wind took them all over. ”
In the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Ghost. Amen.
paragraphs.zh: 以圣父、圣子和圣灵的名义。阿门。

paragraphs.en: What kind of bird is that?
Yeah, I can’t sleep on occasion.
Why? Do you see that big hand pointing a finger at you?
paragraphs.zh: 为什么？你看到那只大手在指着你吗？

paragraphs.en: Was your sermon directed at anyone in particular?
paragraphs.zh: 你的讲道是针对特定的人吗？

paragraphs.en: I received a letter from my brother in Maryland.
paragraphs.zh: 我收到了我在马里兰州的兄弟的一封信。

paragraphs.en: Then maybe you should go and see him.
paragraphs.zh: 那么也许你应该去看看他。

paragraphs.en: I saw you put an undershirt in Donald Miller’s locker.
paragraphs.zh: 我看到你在唐纳德·米勒的储物柜里放了一件汗衫。

paragraphs.en: He left it in the sacristy.
paragraphs.zh: 他把它留在了圣器收藏室里。

paragraphs.en: Why didn’t you hand it to him?
I’m trying to spare him further embarrassment.
paragraphs.zh: 我正在努力避免让他更加尴尬。

paragraphs.en: It’s me that cares about that child, not her.
paragraphs.zh: 关心那个孩子的是我，而不是她。

paragraphs.en: Has she ever reached out a hand?
That black boy needs help or he’s not gonna make it.
paragraphs.zh: 那个黑人男孩需要帮助，否则他就活不下去了。

paragraphs.en: If she has her way, he’ll be left to his own undoing.
paragraphs.zh: 如果她如愿以偿，他就会自食其果。

paragraphs.en: Why do you think he drank the wine?
paragraphs.zh: 你认为他为什么喝这酒？

paragraphs.en: And she sees me talk in a human way to these kids and she immediately assumes that there must be something wrong with it.
paragraphs.zh: 她看到我以人性化的方式与这些孩子交谈，她立即认为肯定有什么问题。

paragraphs.en: Well, I’m not gonna let her keep this parish in the Dark Ages, and I’m not gonna let her destroy my spirit of compassion.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，我不会让她在黑暗时代保留这个教区，我也不会让她摧毁我的同情心。

paragraphs.en: I’m sure that’s not her intent.
paragraphs.zh: 我确信这不是她的意图。

paragraphs.en: That I care about this congregation.
Like you care about your class.
paragraphs.zh: 就像你关心你的班级一样。

paragraphs.en: How else would you relate to children?
paragraphs.zh: 除此之外，你还会如何与孩子相处？

paragraphs.en: Now, I can look at your face and know your philosophy.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，我可以看着你的脸，了解你的哲学。

paragraphs.en: I don’t know. I mean, of course.
paragraphs.zh: 我不知道。我的意思是，当然。

paragraphs.en: There are people who go after your humanity, Sister, that tell you that the light in your heart is a weakness.
paragraphs.zh: 姐妹，有些人追求你的人性，他们告诉你，你心中的光明是一个弱点。

paragraphs.en: It’s an old tactic of cruel people to kill kindness in the name of virtue.
paragraphs.zh: 以美德之名扼杀善良，是残忍之人的古老伎俩。

paragraphs.en: Have you forgotten the message of our Savior?
paragraphs.zh: 你忘记了我们救主的信息吗？

paragraphs.en: I just feel as if everything is upside down.
paragraphs.zh: 我只是觉得一切都颠倒了。

paragraphs.en: There are just times in life when we feel lost. It happens.
paragraphs.zh: 生活中总会有一些时候我们会感到失落。它发生了。

paragraphs.en: That’s also the day that I tripped. I broke my ankle.
paragraphs.zh: 这也是我绊倒的一天。我扭伤了脚踝。

paragraphs.en: That’s wonderful. Who’s the lucky boy?
paragraphs.zh: 太棒了。谁是幸运的男孩？

paragraphs.en: I just gotta tell you something.
paragraphs.zh: 我只是要告诉你一件事。

paragraphs.en: My parents are gonna be so angry at me.
paragraphs.zh: 我的父母会对我很生气。

paragraphs.en: The next one of you that speaks out of turn gets sent right to the principal.
paragraphs.zh: 下一个不按顺序发言的人会被直接送到校长那里。

paragraphs.en: Now the question was, what was
“Give me liberty or give me death!”
paragraphs.zh: “要么给我自由，要么给我死亡！”

paragraphs.en: James Hurley, down to the principal’s office.
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯·赫尔利，到校长办公室。

paragraphs.en: Thought I mighta had the wrong day.
paragraphs.zh: 我以为我可能过错了日子。

paragraphs.en: I was listening to a transistor radio with an earpiece.
paragraphs.zh: 我正在用耳机听晶体管收音机。

paragraphs.en: Look how tiny they’re making them.
paragraphs.zh: 看看他们把它们做得有多小。

paragraphs.en: I confiscated it from one of the students, now I can’t stop listening to it.
paragraphs.zh: 我从一位学生那里没收了它，现在我无法停止听它。

paragraphs.en: Years ago I used to listen to all the news reports, because my husband was in Italy, in the war.
paragraphs.zh: 几年前，我常常听所有的新闻报道，因为我的丈夫在意大利，在战争中。

paragraphs.en: I only have half an hour myself.
I see. Of course. It was a lot to ask.
paragraphs.zh: 我懂了。当然。有很多问题要问。

paragraphs.en: He was upset about getting taken off the altar boys.
paragraphs.zh: 他对被剥夺祭坛侍童的地位感到不安。

paragraphs.en: He said he was caught drinking altar wine.
paragraphs.zh: 他说他喝坛酒被发现了。

paragraphs.en: But he’s a good boy, Sister.
He fell down there, but he’s a good boy pretty much down the line.
paragraphs.zh: 他在那里摔倒了，但他基本上是一个好孩子。

paragraphs.en: His father beat the hell out of him over that wine.
paragraphs.zh: 他父亲因为那酒把他狠狠地打了一顿。

paragraphs.en: You don’t tell my husband what to do.
paragraphs.zh: 你不告诉我丈夫该怎么做。

paragraphs.en: My husband didn’t want Donald to come here.
paragraphs.zh: 我丈夫不想让唐纳德来这里。

paragraphs.en: He thought he’d have trouble with the other boys, but that hasn’t really happened.
paragraphs.zh: 他以为他会和其他男孩产生矛盾，但事实并没有真正发生。

paragraphs.en: That priest, Father Flynn, been watching out for him.
paragraphs.zh: 那位牧师，弗林神父，一直在监视着他。

paragraphs.en: James Hurley, what are you doing out of class?
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯·赫尔利，你课外在做什么？

paragraphs.en: Well, go back up and shut up!
Now go on. Go back to class.
It’s just that I have to walk to work.
paragraphs.zh: 只是我必须步行去上班。

paragraphs.en: If I don’t start cleaning by noon,
paragraphs.zh: 如果我中午之前不开始打扫，

paragraphs.en: I’m not home to let Donald in.
paragraphs.zh: 我不在家让唐纳德进来。
我可以和你一起散步吗？

paragraphs.en: Who can tell me what the common denominator is?
paragraphs.zh: 谁能告诉我共同点是什么？

paragraphs.en: I said, back already, Mr. Hurley?
paragraphs.zh: 我说，赫尔利先生，已经回来了吗？

paragraphs.en: Who do you think you’re talking to?
Just seen him on the altar.
paragraphs.zh: 刚刚在祭坛上看到了他。

paragraphs.en: Haven’t met him face to face.
paragraphs.zh: 还没有和他面对面见过面。

paragraphs.en: No, just, you know, heard from Donald.
paragraphs.zh: 不，只是，你知道，从唐纳德那里听说的。

paragraphs.en: He looks up to him. Man gives him his time, which is what the boy needs.
paragraphs.zh: 他仰视他。男人给了他时间，这正是男孩所需要的。

paragraphs.en: Mrs. Miller, we may have a problem.
paragraphs.zh: 米勒夫人，我们可能遇到麻烦了。

paragraphs.en: I thought there must a been a reason you wanting to see me.
paragraphs.zh: 我想你想见我一定是有原因的。
校长是一项伟大的工作。

paragraphs.en: I just want to say though, it’s just till June.
Donald just has to make it till June.
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德只需坚持到六月即可。

paragraphs.en: Then he’s off into high school.
If Donald can graduate from St. Nicholas, he has a better chance of getting into a good high school, and that would mean an opportunity at college.
paragraphs.zh: 如果唐纳德能够从圣尼古拉斯毕业，他就有更好的机会进入一所好的高中，这意味着有上大学的机会。

paragraphs.en: I don’t see anything at this time standing in the way of his graduating with his class.
paragraphs.zh: 目前我没有看到任何阻碍他随班毕业的事情。

paragraphs.en: Well, that’s all I care about.
I am concerned about the relationship between Father Flynn and your son.
paragraphs.zh: 我很担心弗林神父和你儿子之间的关系。

paragraphs.en: That it may not be right.
Well, there’s something wrong with everybody, and their soul gotta be forgiven.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，每个人都有问题，他们的灵魂必须得到宽恕。

paragraphs.en: I am concerned, to be frank, that Father Flynn may have made advances on your son.
paragraphs.zh: 坦率地说，我担心弗林神父可能对你儿子有过挑衅。

paragraphs.en: Then maybe there’s nothing to it.
I think there is something to it.
I think Father Flynn gave Donald that altar wine.
paragraphs.zh: 我认为弗林神父给了唐纳德那瓶祭坛酒。
唐纳德的行为是否有些奇怪？

paragraphs.en: Look, Sister, I don’t want any trouble.
paragraphs.zh: 听着，姐姐，我不想惹任何麻烦。

paragraphs.en: I don’t… I’m not sure you completely understand.
paragraphs.zh: 我不……我不确定你是否完全理解。

paragraphs.en: I think I understand the kind of thing you’re talking about, but I don’t want to get into it.
paragraphs.zh: 我想我明白你在说什么，但我不想深入其中。

paragraphs.en: Look, I only have a few minutes.
paragraphs.zh: 听着，我只有几分钟的时间。

paragraphs.en: Not to be disagreeing, but if we’re talking about something floating around between this priest and my son, it ain’t my son’s fault.
paragraphs.zh: 并不是要反对，但如果我们谈论的是这位牧师和我儿子之间的一些事情，那不是我儿子的错。

paragraphs.en: If someone should be taking blame, it should be the man…
paragraphs.zh: 如果有人应该承担责任，那应该是男人……

paragraphs.en: I know. I agree with you completely.
paragraphs.zh: 我知道。我完全同意你的观点。

paragraphs.en: You’re agreeing with me, but I got called to the principal, if you know what I’m saying.
paragraphs.zh: 你同意我的观点，但我被叫到了校长，如果你知道我在说什么的话。

paragraphs.en: I am concerned about Donald’s welfare.
You honestly think that priest gave Donald that wine to drink?
paragraphs.zh: 你真的认为牧师给唐纳德喝了酒吗？

paragraphs.en: Then how come my son got kicked off the altar boys if it was a man that gave it to him?
paragraphs.zh: 那么，如果是一个男人给我儿子的，为什么他会被踢出祭坛男孩呢？

paragraphs.en: The boy got caught, the man didn’t.
paragraphs.zh: 男孩被抓住了，而男人却没有。

paragraphs.en: So you’re giving my son the blame.
paragraphs.zh: 所以你把责任归咎于我儿子。

paragraphs.en: No problem my son getting blamed, and you know why that is.
paragraphs.zh: 没问题，我儿子会受到指责，你知道为什么。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps you should let me talk.
Sister, you ain’t going against no man in a robe and win.
paragraphs.zh: 姐妹，你不可能与任何穿着长袍的人对抗并获胜。

paragraphs.en: Do you know what you’re saying?
Know more about it than you.
I believe this man is creating, or may have already brought about, an improper relationship with your son.
paragraphs.zh: 我相信这个人正在或可能已经与你的儿子建立了不正当的关系。

paragraphs.en: Why you gotta know something like that for sure when you don’t?
paragraphs.zh: 为什么你在不知道的情况下却必须确定地知道这样的事情？

paragraphs.en: What kind of mother are you?
paragraphs.zh: 你是一位什么样的母亲？

paragraphs.en: Excuse me, but you don’t know enough about life to say a thing like that, Sister.
paragraphs.zh: 对不起，姐姐，你对生活的了解还不够多，无法说出这样的话。

paragraphs.en: You know the rules maybe, but that don’t cover it.
paragraphs.zh: 您也许知道规则，但这并不涵盖它。

paragraphs.en: I know what I won’t accept.
paragraphs.zh: 我知道我不会接受什么。

paragraphs.en: You accept what you gotta accept, and you work with it.
paragraphs.zh: 你接受你必须接受的东西，并与之合作。

paragraphs.en: This man is in my school.
Well, he’s gotta be somewhere and maybe he’s doing some good, too.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，他一定在某个地方，也许他也在做一些好事。

paragraphs.en: Well, maybe some of them boys want to get caught!
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，也许其中一些男孩想被抓住！

paragraphs.en: That’s why his father beat him.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是他父亲打他的原因。

paragraphs.en: I’m talking about the boy’s nature now, not anything he’s done.
paragraphs.zh: 我现在谈论的是这个男孩的本性，而不是他所做的任何事情。

paragraphs.en: You can’t hold a child responsible for what God gave him to be.
paragraphs.zh: 你不能让孩子对上帝赋予他的职责负责。

paragraphs.en: But then there’s the boy’s nature.
You’re the one forcing people to say things.
paragraphs.zh: 你是那个强迫人们说话的人。

paragraphs.en: My boy came to your school
’cause they were gonna kill him in the public school.
paragraphs.zh: 因为他们要在公立学校杀了他。

paragraphs.en: He come to your school, kids don’t like him.
paragraphs.zh: 他来你们学校，孩子们不喜欢他。

paragraphs.en: One man is good to him, this priest.
paragraphs.zh: 有一个人对他很好，就是这位牧师。

paragraphs.en: Then does a man have his reasons, yes.
paragraphs.zh: 那么一个人有他的理由吗，是的。

paragraphs.en: You have your reasons, but do I ask the man why he’s good to my son?
paragraphs.zh: 你有你的理由，但我有问那个男人为什么对我儿子好吗？

paragraphs.en: My son needs some man to care about him and to see him through to where he wants to go.
paragraphs.zh: 我的儿子需要一个男人来关心他并帮助他到达他想去的地方。

paragraphs.en: And thank God this educated man with some kindness in him wants to do just that.
paragraphs.zh: 感谢上帝，这个受过教育、心怀仁慈的人也想这么做。

paragraphs.en: I’ll throw your son out of this school.
paragraphs.zh: 我会把你儿子赶出这所学校。

paragraphs.en: And why would you do that if it didn’t start with him?
paragraphs.zh: 如果这不是从他开始的，你为什么要这么做呢？

paragraphs.en: You’d hurt my son to get your way?
paragraphs.zh: 你为了达到你的目的而伤害我儿子吗？

paragraphs.en: It won’t end with your son.
paragraphs.zh: 这不会以你的儿子结束。

paragraphs.en: I am trying to do just that.
paragraphs.zh: 我正在努力做到这一点。

paragraphs.en: Then what do you want from me?
paragraphs.zh: 那你想从我这里得到什么？

paragraphs.en: Please leave my son out of this.
paragraphs.zh: 请让我儿子远离这件事。

paragraphs.en: My husband will kill that child over a thing like this.
paragraphs.zh: 我丈夫会因为这样的事情杀了那个孩子。

paragraphs.en: I don’t know if you and me on the same side.
paragraphs.zh: 我不知道你和我是否站在同一边。

paragraphs.en: I’ll be standing with my son and those who are good with my son.
paragraphs.zh: 我会和我的儿子以及那些对我儿子好的人站在一起。

paragraphs.en: It’d be nice to see you there.
A third party would be required.
Yeah. What was Donald’s mother doing here?
paragraphs.zh: 是的。唐纳德的母亲在这里做什么？

paragraphs.en: A third party would truly be required, Father.
Me and you are due for a talk!
You have to stop this campaign against me.
paragraphs.zh: 你必须停止针对我的这场运动。

paragraphs.en: You can stop it at any time.
You are attempting to destroy my reputation!
paragraphs.zh: 你这是企图败坏我的名誉！

paragraphs.en: What are you doing in this school?
Even more to the point, what are you doing in the priesthood?
paragraphs.zh: 更重要的是，你在圣职中做什么？

paragraphs.en: You are single-handedly holding this school and this parish back.
paragraphs.zh: 你单枪匹马地阻碍了这所学校和这个教区的发展。

paragraphs.en: Progressive education and a welcoming church.
paragraphs.zh: 进步的教育和热情的教堂。
你不能分散我的注意力，神父。

paragraphs.en: This is not about my behavior, it’s about yours.
paragraphs.zh: 这与我的行为无关，而是与你的行为有关。

paragraphs.en: No, this is about your unfounded suspicions.
paragraphs.zh: 不，这是你毫无根据的怀疑。

paragraphs.en: Just leave that. It’s not important.

paragraphs.zh: 我会决定什么是重要的。

paragraphs.en: You gave that boy wine, and you let him take the blame.
paragraphs.zh: 你给了那个男孩酒，你让他承担责任。

paragraphs.en: Did you talk to Mr. McGuinn?
paragraphs.zh: 你和麦吉恩先生谈过吗？

paragraphs.en: All McGuinn knows is that the boy drank wine.
paragraphs.zh: 麦吉恩只知道这个男孩喝了酒。

paragraphs.en: He doesn’t know how he came to drink it.
paragraphs.zh: 他不知道自己是怎么喝到它的。

paragraphs.en: Did his mother have something to add to that?
paragraphs.zh: 他的母亲还有什么要补充的吗？

paragraphs.en: You’ve got it in your head that I corrupted this child after giving him wine, and nothing I say will change that.
paragraphs.zh: 你心里已经认定，我给这孩子喝酒后就腐蚀了他，无论我说什么都改变不了这一点。

paragraphs.en: But this has nothing to do with the wine.
Not really. You’ve had a fundamental mistrust of me before this incident.
paragraphs.zh: 并不真地。在这件事发生之前，你已经对我产生了根本的不信任。

paragraphs.en: It was you that warned Sister James to be on the lookout, wasn’t it?
paragraphs.zh: 是你警告詹姆斯修女要小心，不是吗？

paragraphs.en: You will tell me what you have done.
paragraphs.zh: 你会告诉我你做了什么。

paragraphs.en: Sister James is convinced I’m innocent.
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯修女确信我是无辜的。

paragraphs.en: So you talked to Sister James.
paragraphs.zh: 所以你和詹姆斯修女谈过。

paragraphs.en: Well, of course you talked to Sister James.
paragraphs.zh: 嗯，当然你和詹姆斯修女谈过。

paragraphs.en: And might that not account for the odd behavior
paragraphs.zh: 这也许不能解释奇怪的行为

paragraphs.en: Sister James noticed in the boy?
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯修女注意到这个男孩了吗？

paragraphs.en: What did you see that convinced you so thoroughly?
paragraphs.zh: 你看到了什么让你如此彻底地相信？

paragraphs.en: It matters! What does it matter?
paragraphs.zh: 这很重要！这有什么关系呢？

paragraphs.en: I saw you grab William London’s wrist, and I saw him pull away.
paragraphs.zh: 我看到你抓住威廉·伦敦的手腕，然后我看到他挣脱开来。

paragraphs.en: I’m writing down what you say.
It might be important when I have to explain why you have to be removed.
paragraphs.zh: 当我必须解释为什么你必须被开除时，这可能很重要。

paragraphs.en: This morning before I spoke with Mrs. Miller,
paragraphs.zh: 今天早上，在我和米勒夫人谈话之前，

paragraphs.en: I took the precaution of calling your last parish.
paragraphs.zh: 我采取了预防措施，给你最后一个教区打电话。

paragraphs.en: I did not speak to the pastor.
You should have spoken to the pastor.
You know that’s not the proper route for you to have taken, Sister.
paragraphs.zh: 姐妹，你知道这不是你该走的正确路线。

paragraphs.en: The church is very clear. You’re supposed to go through the pastor.
paragraphs.zh: 教堂里的情况非常清楚。你应该经过牧师。

paragraphs.en: Why? You have an understanding, you and he?
paragraphs.zh: 为什么？你和他有默契吗？

paragraphs.en: No, you have no right to go rummaging through my past!
paragraphs.zh: 不，你没有权利翻查我的过去！

paragraphs.en: This is your third parish in five years.
paragraphs.zh: 这是五年内您的第三个教区。

paragraphs.en: Go after another child and another child, until you are stopped.
paragraphs.zh: 追着一个又一个的孩子，直到被拦住。

paragraphs.en: What nun did you speak to?
Yeah, I’ve not touched a child.
You haven’t the slightest proof of anything.
I’ll go to your last parish and the one before that.
paragraphs.zh: 我会去你的最后一个教区和之前的一个。

paragraphs.en: If necessary, I’ll find a parent.
paragraphs.zh: 如果有必要的话，我会找家长。

paragraphs.en: Trust me, Father Flynn, I will.
paragraphs.zh: 相信我，弗林神父，我会的。

paragraphs.en: You have no right to act on your own!
You have taken vows, obedience being one!
paragraphs.zh: 你们已发誓，服从就是一！

paragraphs.en: You have no right to step outside the church!
I will step outside the church if that’s what needs to be done, though the door should shut behind me!
paragraphs.zh: 如果需要的话，我会走出教堂，尽管门应该在我身后关上！

paragraphs.en: I will do what needs to be done, though I’m damned to hell!
paragraphs.zh: 我会做需要做的事情，尽管我该死！

paragraphs.en: You should understand that, or you will mistake me.
paragraphs.zh: 你应该明白这一点，否则你会误会我。

paragraphs.en: Now, did you give Donald Miller wine to drink?
paragraphs.zh: 现在，你给唐纳德·米勒喝葡萄酒了吗？

paragraphs.en: Have you never done anything wrong?
paragraphs.zh: 你从来没有做过错事吗？

paragraphs.en: I have left in the healing hands of my confessor.
paragraphs.zh: 我已经交到了我忏悔神父的治愈之手。

paragraphs.en: No, we are not. We are not the same.
paragraphs.zh: 不，我们不是。我们不一样。

paragraphs.en: A dog that bites is a dog that bites.
paragraphs.zh: 咬人的狗就是咬人的狗。

paragraphs.en: Did you give Donald Miller wine to drink?
paragraphs.zh: 你给唐纳德·米勒喝过酒吗？

paragraphs.en: If you will not leave my office, I will.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你不离开我的办公室，我就会离开。

paragraphs.en: And once I go, I will not stop.
paragraphs.zh: 一旦我走了，我就不会停下来。

paragraphs.en: I can’t say everything, you understand?
paragraphs.zh: 我不能说出一切，你明白吗？

paragraphs.en: There’s things I can’t say, even if you can’t imagine the explanation, Sister.
paragraphs.zh: 有些事情我不能说，即使你无法想象解释，姐妹。

paragraphs.en: Remember, there are things beyond your knowledge.
paragraphs.zh: 请记住，有些事情超出了您的知识范围。

paragraphs.en: Even if you feel certainty, it is an emotion, not a fact.
paragraphs.zh: 即使你感到确定，那也是一种情绪，而不是事实。

paragraphs.en: You will request a transfer and take a leave of absence until it’s granted.
paragraphs.zh: 您将请求转学并请假，直到获得批准为止。
你不会给我留下任何东西。

paragraphs.en: It’s Donald Miller who has nothing, and you took full advantage of that.
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德·米勒一无所有，而你充分利用了这一点。

paragraphs.en: And you talk to him as if you were the same?
paragraphs.zh: 你和他说话就像你是一样的吗？

paragraphs.en: And that warm feeling you experienced when that boy looked at you with trust was not the sensation of virtue.
paragraphs.zh: 当那个男孩用信任的眼神看着你时，你感受到的那种温暖的感觉并不是德行的感觉。

paragraphs.en: That could be got by any drunkard with his tot of rum.
paragraphs.zh: 任何一个醉汉只要喝点朗姆酒就可以得到这个。

paragraphs.en: Nowhere you can get at it.

paragraphs.zh: 留在这里。让自己镇定下来。

paragraphs.en: You can use the phone if you’d like.
paragraphs.zh: 如果您愿意，可以使用电话。

paragraphs.en: I have no sympathy for you.
I know you are invulnerable to true regret.
paragraphs.zh: 我知道你不会受到真正的遗憾的影响。

paragraphs.en: Praise God, from whom all blessings flow
paragraphs.zh: 赞美神，一切祝福都从他而来

paragraphs.en: Praise him all creatures here below
paragraphs.zh: 赞美他下面所有的生物

paragraphs.en: Praise him above, ye heavenly host
paragraphs.zh: 天军啊，请在上面赞美他

paragraphs.en: Praise Father, Son and Holy Ghost
I never like to say goodbye, but there is a wind behind every one of us that takes us through our lives.
paragraphs.zh: 我从来不喜欢说再见，但我们每个人背后都有一股风，带我们走完人生。

paragraphs.en: We don’t even know its purpose.
paragraphs.zh: 我们甚至不知道它的目的。

paragraphs.en: ’Cause I would have stayed among you longer, but that wind is taking me away.
paragraphs.zh: 因为我本来可以在你们中间呆得更久，但那股风正在把我带走。

paragraphs.en: I will miss it here, and I will miss you.
paragraphs.zh: 我会想念这里，我也会想念你。

paragraphs.en: But I’m content that the power that propels me does so with superior knowledge as to what is for the best, and that is my faith.
paragraphs.zh: 但我很满意，推动我这样做的力量是通过卓越的知识来实现的，这就是我的信念。

paragraphs.en: I would like to come down now among you and say goodbye and wish you peaceful hearts and loving hearts.
paragraphs.zh: 我现在想来到你们中间，向你们道别，并祝愿你们拥有平静的心和充满爱心的心。

paragraphs.en: Thank you very much. Thank you.

paragraphs.zh: 一首古老而优美的歌谣

paragraphs.en: From angels bending near the Earth
paragraphs.zh: 来自靠近地球弯曲的天使

paragraphs.en: You were missed. How is your brother?
paragraphs.zh: 你被错过了。你哥哥好吗？

paragraphs.en: I’m very glad. I prayed for him.
It was good to get away.
I needed to see my family.
Mmm. Then I’m glad you did it.
paragraphs.zh: 嗯。那我很高兴你做到了。

paragraphs.en: So you did it. You got him out.
paragraphs.zh: 所以你做到了。你把他赶了出来。

paragraphs.en: Can’t be helped. It’s just till June.
I don’t think Father Flynn did anything wrong.
paragraphs.zh: 我不认为弗林神父做错了什么。

paragraphs.en: I wish I could be like you.
Maybe we’re not supposed to sleep so well.
paragraphs.zh: 也许我们不应该睡得那么好。

paragraphs.en: They made Father Flynn pastor of Saint Jerome.
paragraphs.zh: 他们任命弗林神父为圣杰罗姆的牧师。

paragraphs.en: Appointed Father Flynn pastor of Saint Jerome Church and School.
paragraphs.zh: 任命弗林神父为圣杰罗姆教堂和学校牧师。

paragraphs.en: Oh, I told our good monsignor.
paragraphs.zh: 哦，我告诉了我们的好主教。

paragraphs.en: I crossed the garden, and I told him.
He did not believe it to be true.
Then why did Father Flynn go?
paragraphs.zh: 那么弗林神父为什么要走呢？

paragraphs.en: What did you say to make him leave?
That I had called a nun in his previous parish, that I had found out his prior history of infringements.
paragraphs.zh: 我打电话给他以前教区的一位修女，我发现了他以前的侵权历史。
我没有打过这样的电话。

paragraphs.en: But if he’d had no such history, the lie wouldn’t have worked.
paragraphs.zh: 但如果他没有这样的历史，这个谎言就不会起作用。
他的辞职就是他的忏悔。

paragraphs.en: He was what I thought he was.
paragraphs.zh: 他就是我所认为的那样。

paragraphs.en: In the pursuit of wrongdoing, one steps away from God.
paragraphs.zh: 在追求不法行为的过程中，一个人就远离了上帝。

paragraphs.en: Of course, there is a price.
Come, thou, redeemer of the Earth

paragraphs.zh: 让每一个时代的崇拜都堕落

paragraphs.en: Such birth befits the God of all
paragraphs.zh: 这样的诞生适合所有人的上帝

paragraphs.en: But of the Spirit, thou art still
The word of God in flesh arrayed
The promised fruit to man displayed
paragraphs.zh: 向人类展示的应许果子

paragraphs.en: The virgin womb that burden gained

paragraphs.zh: 带着处女般的荣誉，一切都未受玷污

paragraphs.en: The banners there of virtue glow
paragraphs.zh: 那里美德的旗帜闪闪发光

paragraphs.en: God in his temple dwells below
Forth from his chamber goeth he
A giant in twofold substance one
Rejoicing now his course to run
paragraphs.zh: 现在很高兴他的路线正在运行

paragraphs.en: From God the Father he proceeds
To God the Father back he speeds
His course he runs to death and hell
paragraphs.zh: 他的路线是走向死亡和地狱

paragraphs.en: Returning on God’s throne to dwell
O equal to thy Father, thou
Gird on thy fleshly mantle now
paragraphs.zh: 现在穿上你的肉身斗篷

paragraphs.en: The weakness of our mortal state
Thy cradle here shall glitter bright
paragraphs.zh: 你的摇篮在这里将闪闪发光

paragraphs.en: And darkness breathe a newer light
Where endless faith shall shine serene
paragraphs.zh: 无尽的信仰将在宁静中闪耀

paragraphs.en: All praise, eternal Son, to thee
paragraphs.zh: 永恒之子，一切赞美归于你

paragraphs.en: Whose advent sets thy people free
paragraphs.zh: 谁的出现让你的人民获得了自由

paragraphs.en: Whom, with the Father, we adore
paragraphs.zh: 我们与天父一起崇拜他
